attacking_style: Employs late-race attacks through domestiques to destabilize rivals rather than direct GC rider offensives. Opportunistic acceleration when other teams initiate, suggesting reactive rather than primary aggression.
team_tactics: Flexible approach between full-stage pacing for GC defense and strategic domestique attacks to force rival teams into defensive chasing. Positioning-based control preferred over sustained tempo when defending small margins.
terrain_preference: Insufficient data for definitive terrain preference. Observations span mixed parcours showing adaptability to both stage race GC defense and opportunistic scenarios.
vulnerability: Appears to lack consistent offensive firepower for sustained tempo control in high-mountain scenarios. Relies on tactical disruption rather than dominant climbing strength to influence GC races.
key_patterns: Uses domestique attacks (De la Cruz example) to isolate rivals and shift defensive burden.
Strategic pacing deployment: full-stage control when necessary, positioning-only when margin allows efficiency.
Reactive team tactics: joins rival accelerations rather than consistently dictating race pace themselves.
form_trajectory: unknown: observations span 2018-2021 without sufficient recent concentrated data to assess current trajectory
